message:
fix(fcp-sandbox): reject invalid CIDR deny rules instead of silently ignoring them

The egress guard previously used an unchecked `if let Ok()` pattern when
parsing CIDR deny rules, which silently swallowed parse errors. A
malformed CIDR string like "not-a-cidr" would be treated as a no-match
rather than a configuration error, potentially allowing unintended
network traffic through the sandbox egress filter.

Switch to an explicit `match` that returns an error on CIDR parse
failure with a descriptive message including the offending rule and
the underlying parse error. This ensures misconfigured egress policies
are caught at evaluation time rather than silently degrading security.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
